title: QA Engineer
description:
Job Descriptions

1. Quality Control & Inspection:

  • Perform detailed QC buy-off inspections of precision-engineered parts, assemblies, or systems at various stages of production, utilizing industry-standard inspection techniques (e.g., CMM, visual inspection, functional testing).
  • Track non-conformance reports filed during the buy-off process and develop and implement preventive action plans to mitigate recurring defects.

2. Process Development

  • Design and implement quality assurance protocols, checklists, and testing procedures specific to precision engineering workflows.
  • Establish, update, and ensure adherence to quality documentation, including inspection reports and non-conformance records.
  • Coordinate and oversee the First Article Inspection (FAI) process for new manufacturing processes or parts, ensuring compliance with industry and customer standards.

3. Collaboration & Problem Solving

  • Work closely with design engineers to review technical drawings, specifications, and tolerances, providing feedback to enhance manufacturability and quality.
  • Drive cross-functional initiatives to enhance long-term quality and reliability, performing root cause analysis and implementing corrective actions.
  • Lead the tracking and investigation of quality-related returns and customer complaints using the 8D methodology, coordinating problem-solving efforts and implementing long-term preventive measures.

4. Compliance & Standards

  • Ensure all products meet industry standards (e.g., ISO 9001, ASME, or other relevant certifications) and customer requirements.
  • Participate in internal and external audits, ensuring adherence to regulatory and supplier quality standards.

5. Continuous Improvement

  • Analyze quality data and trends from issue tracking systems (Project Issue Log) to identify opportunities for process optimization and defect reduction. Classify issues into relevant disciplines such as quality, operational, or design-related concerns.
  • Host post-mortem discussions after project closures, documenting lessons learned and integrating preventive action plans into future workflows.

6. Documentation & Reporting

  • Maintain accurate records of inspections, test results, and quality metrics using digital Quality Management Systems (QMS).
  • Prepare detailed reports for management and clients on quality performance, compliance status, and improvement initiatives, providing actionable insights to drive quality excellence."

7. Any other Task Assigned by Direct reporting manager or Stage BU head.


Requirements

  • Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Precision Engineering, Quality Management, or a related field from a recognized institution.
  • Preferably 3-5 years of experience in quality control or quality assurance within precision engineering or manufacturing, with hands-on expertise in inspection techniques (e.g. CMM, visual inspection, functional testing).

Skill

  • Proficiency in interpreting technical drawings, specifications, and tolerances.
  • Experience with Quality Management Systems (QMS) and issue tracking tools (e.g., Project Issue Log).
  • Familiarity with First Article Inspection (FAI), root cause analysis (e.g., 8D methodology), and non-conformance reporting

Personal Attributes

  • Detail-Oriented: Exceptional attention to detail to ensure precision in inspections, documentation, and compliance with stringent quality standards.
  • Analytical Thinker: Strong problem-solving skills with a methodical approach to conducting root cause analysis and implementing effective corrective actions.
  • Collaborative Team Player: Ability to work seamlessly with cross-functional teams, including design engineers and production staff, fostering a cooperative and solution-driven environment.
  • Proactive and Initiative-Driven: A self-starter who anticipates quality issues, develops preventive measures, and drives continuous improvement without needing constant supervision.
  • Adaptable: Flexible and resilient, able to handle evolving priorities and additional tasks assigned by management in a fast-paced manufacturing setting.
  • Communicative: Clear and concise in conveying quality findings, preparing reports, and presenting insights to stakeholders at all levels.
  • Results-Focused: Committed to achieving high-quality outcomes, meeting deadlines, and ensuring customer satisfaction through reliable processes.
  • Ethical and Integrity-Driven: Upholds the highest standards of professionalism and accountability, ensuring compliance with industry regulations and organizational policies.
  • Calm Under Pressure: Maintains composure and sound judgment during audits, customer complaints, or production challenges, ensuring effective resolution.

districts.location: D19 Serangoon Garden, Hougang, Sengkang, Punggol
